name: Cabbage (food medium)

components.name: water
components.evidence: Moisture of raw cabbage; the matrix is essentially a dilute aqueous leaf tissue.

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 169975; Rosa et al. 2001 J Sci Food Agric doi:10.1002/jsfa.919
components.evidence: Glucose is the dominant free sugar in cabbage and the principal fermentable carbon source.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 169975; Rosa et al. 2001 doi:10.1002/jsfa.919
components.evidence: Second most abundant free sugar (~equal to glucose).

components.name: sucrose
components.evidence: Minor disaccharide (~11% of total sugars).

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Trace.

components.name: L-malic acid
components.source: Fresh/fermenting white-cabbage organic-acid study (ResearchGate fig tbl2 id 338031753; fresh total titratable acidity ~0.4%); Brassica HPLC surveys (malic predominant)
components.evidence: Malic acid is the predominant organic acid of fresh cabbage; midpoint of reported ~150-300 mg/100 g. A dominant fermentable carbon/energy source and malolactic substrate. WAS ABSENT from FooDB record.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Fresh cabbage organic-acid literature; Brassica sprouts profile citric>malic>oxalic (Food Control 2015 doi:10.1016/j.foodcont.2015.02.035)
components.evidence: Second major organic acid; ~50-100 mg/100 g range, midpoint used. WAS ABSENT from FooDB record.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Oxalate-content reviews (cabbage classed low-oxalate, ~4-7 mg/100 g fw)
components.evidence: Low-oxalate Brassica; midpoint ~5 mg/100 g. WAS ABSENT from FooDB record.

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: HPLC organic-acid surveys of Brassica oleracea
components.evidence: Present as a minor organic acid, not reliably quantified for fresh head cabbage.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: HPLC organic-acid surveys of Brassica oleracea
components.evidence: Trace, present but unquantified.

components.name: quinic acid
components.evidence: Present (co-eluting with malic in some methods); unquantified for fresh cabbage.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Total crude protein; amino-acid profile below is protein-derived plus free pool.

components.name: L-tryptophan
components.evidence: Protein-bound + free.

components.name: L-aspartate
components.evidence: Includes asparagine.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (incl. glutamine); free glutamate is a key N source and umami contributor.

components.name: D-serine
components.evidence: Free D-serine reported in FooDB; minor, not separately quantified (L-serine value above is the protein-derived pool).

components.name: gamma-aminobutyrate (GABA)
components.evidence: Present as a free amino acid; unquantified.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: Cabbage is a very low-fat matrix.

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Dominant saturated FA.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Small ALA fraction; the dominant PUFA of leaf (galacto)lipids.

components.name: L-ascorbate (vitamin C)
components.evidence: High vitamin C; also an organic acid. Note lower 13-30 mg/100g in some cultivar surveys (PMC7464038).

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Not in USDA panel; approximate value.

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: Total folate.

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Total choline.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Provitamin A carotenoid (vitamin A ~5 ug RAE).

components.name: phylloquinone (vitamin K1)
components.evidence: Notably high for a pale leaf vegetable.

components.name: potassium
components.evidence: Dominant cation.

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: As phosphate; key P source.

components.name: sulfur
components.evidence: Cabbage is sulfur-rich (glucosinolates, S-methylmethionine); total S / sulfate not in USDA panel.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Present counter-ion; unquantified in USDA panel.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Consolidates FooDB 'Iron', 'Iron (Fe3+)', 'Fe2+ mitochondria' duplicates into one value.

components.name: molybdenum
components.evidence: Genuine ultratrace micronutrient (nitrate reductase cofactor); unquantified in USDA panel.

components.name: nickel
components.evidence: Ultratrace micronutrient (urease cofactor); unquantified. Retained rather than dropped as a genuine trace nutrient.

components.name: ammonium
components.evidence: Free ammonium present in leaf tissue; a directly assimilable inorganic N source. Unquantified.

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate

remove.name: Fluorine

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: ultratrace cobalt placeholder; not growth-relevant and not quantified (plant food, no B12)

remove.name: Nitrogen
remove.reason: N2 gas placeholder, not a compositional nutrient of the food

remove.name: CO2 CO2
remove.reason: gas placeholder, not a compositional nutrient of the food

remove.name: O2 O2
remove.reason: gas placeholder, not a food nutrient

remove.name: H+
remove.reason: proton placeholder, not a compositional nutrient

remove.name: Aniline
remove.reason: not a food nutrient; predicted/contaminant amine, implausible as a growth substrate

remove.name: N-Methylaniline
remove.reason: predicted/contaminant amine, not a genuine cabbage nutrient

remove.name: N-Methylphenethylamine
remove.reason: predicted trace amine, not a defined growth nutrient

remove.name: Benzylamine
remove.reason: predicted trace amine, negligible

remove.name: 2-Phenylethylamine
remove.reason: trace biogenic amine, negligible as a growth nutrient

remove.name: Ethanamine
remove.reason: predicted trace amine, negligible

remove.name: Pentylamine
remove.reason: predicted trace amine, negligible

remove.name: Ethyl methyl amine
remove.reason: predicted trace amine, negligible

remove.name: Methylamine
remove.reason: trace amine, not a defined growth nutrient

remove.name: Dimethylamine
remove.reason: trace amine, not a defined growth nutrient

missing_added: L-malic acid (dominant organic acid, was absent from FooDB record)
succinic, fumaric, quinic acid (present but unquantified)
individual free sugars with amounts (glucose ~= fructose >> sucrose)
full vitamin panel with amounts (B1/B2/B3/B5/B6/B7/B9/C/E/K, choline, beta-carotene)
consolidated iron (removed organelle/oxidation-state duplicates); added Na/K/Ca/Mg/P/Zn/Mn/Cu/Se amounts
free ammonium retained as an inorganic N source
keep_uncovered: S-Methyl-L-methionine chloride (SMM / 'vitamin U', characteristic cabbage compound)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169975 (Cabbage, raw) - full sugar/AA/FA/vitamin/mineral panel
Rosa et al. 2001, J Sci Food Agric, doi:10.1002/jsfa.919 (glucose/fructose/sucrose in cabbage)
PMC7464038 Comparative Evaluation of Bioactive Compounds and Volatile Profile of White Cabbages (sugars, ascorbate, sinapic acid)
Fresh/fermenting white-cabbage organic-acid study (ResearchGate fig tbl2 id 338031753; fresh total titratable acidity ~0.4%, malic/citric predominant)
Brassica oleracea sprouts organic-acid profile, Food Control 2015 doi:10.1016/j.foodcont.2015.02.035 (citric>malic>oxalic dominance)
Oxalate-content reviews classifying cabbage as low-oxalate (~4-7 mg/100 g fw)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for growth are the free sugars glucose (~1.67 g) and fructose (~1.45 g); L-malic acid (~0.24 g) is the principal organic acid and a malolactic substrate, with citric acid secondary and oxalic acid low. Unlike fruits, sugars far outweigh organic acids here. Nitrogen comes from a full amino-acid pool (glutamate/glutamine-rich) plus free ammonium. Very low fat. Organic-acid amounts are literature midpoints (medium confidence); succinic/fumaric/quinic present but unquantified. FooDB noise removed: heavy metals (Ag, As, Cd, Pb, Hg), rare-earths/ultratrace elements, gas and proton placeholders, and predicted/contaminant amines (aniline, N-methylaniline, alkylamines). Consistent with peer curation food_FOOD00887 (white cabbage), same USDA source.
